course.actionPublishConfirmBody: The course will move to In progress and show as active.
course.actionFinalizeConfirmBody: The course will be marked as Finished. You can reopen it later if needed.

users.emptyHint: Create the first user to get started.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Connect your calendar account (Google today, Outlook coming soon) and sync your course sessions as events with Google Meet, automatically inviting enrolled students.
calendar.providerHint: Google Calendar is supported today. Outlook / Microsoft 365 is on the roadmap — same integration shape.
calendar.connectBody: You'll be redirected to the provider to authorize access. Only you have access to your tokens — each teacher connects their own and the app never exposes them to the client.
calendar.errorNoRefresh: The provider didn't return a refresh token. Revoke ExamLab access from your account settings and reconnect.
calendar.connectedToastGoogle: Google Calendar connected successfully — you can now pick a target calendar and sync your sessions.
calendar.connectedToastMicrosoft: Outlook / Microsoft 365 connected successfully — you can now pick a target calendar and sync your sessions.
calendar.disconnectBody: You're about to remove access to your calendar. Already-synced events stay in your calendar; upcoming sessions won't sync until you reconnect.
calendar.disconnectedToastGoogle: Google Calendar disconnected — events already created stay in your calendar, but ExamLab can no longer update them.
calendar.disconnectedToastMicrosoft: Outlook / Microsoft 365 disconnected — events already created stay in your calendar, but ExamLab can no longer update them.
calendar.step1Body: Pick which of your calendars will receive the session events.
calendar.saveCalendarFirst: Pick and save a target calendar first.
calendar.step2Body: Create/update an event for each course attendance session, with a Google Meet link, and invite every enrolled student (institutional email). Sessions with an existing event are updated; new ones are created.
calendar.noCoursesWithTime: No courses ready to sync. Every session must have a start time and duration before the course can sync with your calendar. Set them from the course board (Courses → board → edit session).
calendar.syncDoneToast: Sync complete — {{created}} created, {{updated}} updated{{failed, plural, =0{} other {, {{failed}} failed}}}
calendar.syncFootnote: Events use the actual start time and duration of each session. If you didn't set a time, it falls back to 09:00 Bogotá with 90 min.

pptxViewer.subtitle: Slide-by-slide view of the AI-generated material. Tweak small details without regenerating, download as .pptx, or call the AI again if you're not happy.
pptxViewer.editingSubtitle: Edit mode: adjust title and bullets per slide. One bullet per line. After saving, the next download reflects the changes.
pptxViewer.empty: No slides detected in the file body. The .pptx might be empty or in an unrecognized format.
pptxViewer.savedToast: Changes saved — the next download reflects the edited content.
pptxViewer.regenerateHint: Re-generate this file with AI, discarding manual edits. The rest of the course remains intact.
pptxViewer.errorEmpty: Cannot save an empty file. Cancel the changes or add at least one slide.

mediaViewer.replaceMustPdf: The replacement must be a PDF.

confirmEmailChange.errorExpired: The link expired (valid for 1h). Request the change again from your profile.
confirmEmailChange.errorCancelled: This change was cancelled from the previous email. If it was you, request a new one from your profile.
confirmEmailChange.errorAlreadyConfirmed: This change was already confirmed and applied. If you need to revert it, use the link in the notice email we sent to the previous address (valid for 24h).
confirmEmailChange.errorEmailTaken: The target email is already in use by another account. Request the change again with a different address.
confirmEmailChange.errorMissingToken: The link doesn't contain the confirmation token. Make sure you opened the full email.
confirmEmailChange.errorGeneric: The link is invalid or has already been used. Request a new one from your profile if you still need to change your email.
confirmEmailChange.title: Confirm email change
confirmEmailChange.subtitle: We're validating your link to update your account email.
confirmEmailChange.appliedTitle: Change applied — your email was updated
confirmEmailChange.appliedBody: From now on you sign in with this email:
confirmEmailChange.revertHint: We sent a notice to your previous email with a link to revert for security. If it wasn't you, use that link within 24h.

cancelEmailChange.errorWindowExpired: More than 24h have passed since the change; the window to revert from this link has closed. If you don't recognize the change, contact support and reset your password.
cancelEmailChange.errorLegacyToken: This link corresponds to a change made before the new revert flow. The change was cancelled, but we can't restore it automatically. If you need your previous email, contact support.
cancelEmailChange.errorPreviousTaken: Your previous email was taken by another account. We can't revert automatically — contact support to resolve it.
cancelEmailChange.errorMissingToken: The link doesn't contain the cancellation token.
cancelEmailChange.errorGeneric: The link is invalid or expired. If you think this is an account takeover attempt, change your password.
cancelEmailChange.title: Cancel email change
cancelEmailChange.subtitle: We're processing the cancellation of the requested change.
cancelEmailChange.successRevertBody: Your account is using your previous email again. Sign in with it:
cancelEmailChange.successBody: Your account email will NOT be changed. Your access keeps working with your current email.
cancelEmailChange.securityHint: If you did NOT request this change, someone may have compromised your password. Change it now from the login (forgot your password?).

columns.statusHint: Possible states: 'In progress' = active attempt; 'Completed' = submitted on time without alerts; 'Suspicious' = exceeded the max strikes (proctoring) or AI detected high fraud probability; 'Checked' = a suspicious/copied attempt that the teacher reviewed and marked as OK.
columns.dialogHint: Amber: open conversations with the student. Red: conversations awaiting your reply. Click to open the comments panel.

prompts.removeOverrideBody: This course will revert to the global prompt. You can create another override later if needed.

contents.title: Contents
contents.subtitle: Generate AI-powered presentations and teacher guides
contents.emptyTitle: You haven't generated any content yet.
contents.emptyHint: Create a full course or a single session in seconds with AI.
contents.topicHint: Be specific: "Control structures in Python" produces better material than "Python". For a full course, this topic threads through all sessions.
contents.modeHint: Full course: AI generates the intro + N numbered sessions with _CLASE_N suffix (you can assign each session to attendance). Single session: just one session.
contents.nClassesHint: Number of course sessions. AI will generate a full set (slides + guide + workshop per modality) for each. Usually matches the number of planned attendance sessions.
contents.modalityHint: Defines what files AI produces. Theory: only slides + teacher guide (lectures). Practice: only the step-by-step workshop (labs). Theory + practice: all three (most common).
contents.modeFullDesc: Generates intro + N sessions with slides and teacher guide
contents.durationHelper: 10–480 minutes. Defines the extension of generated material.
contents.tagsHint: Mix freely: theory generates slides + guide; practice adds a workshop; exam adds a per-session exam (hidden from students).
contents.tagsRequired: Pick at least one content type.
contents.scopeWholeCourseHint: One assessment with no cut assigned.
contents.scopePerCutHint: Creates one assessment per course cut.
contents.scopePerCutNoCuts: The course has no cuts defined.
contents.author: Author (optional)
contents.instructionsPlaceholder: e.g. focus the workshop on data engineering, avoid finance examples, use Latin American Spanish terminology, include an analogy with the public transport system…
contents.instructionsHint: Free text appended to the prompt when running generation. Useful to refine the output without changing the global Contents prompt. The AI treats these as high-priority over the defaults.
contents.queuedHint: Generation can take 30–90 seconds. You can close this window — files will appear in the list when ready.
contents.deleteBody: The content and all its generated files will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
contents.deleteFileBody: Only this file will be removed from the content (storage + reference). Other files in the content stay intact. This action cannot be undone.
contents.regenerateClassHint: Re-generate ONLY this class's material. The intro and other classes are kept.
contents.createdToast: Generation started — you'll see the files when done
contents.viewFilesByClassSubtitleCourse: Full course with {{count}} session(s). Each session groups its downloadable files (slides, teacher guide, workshop).
contents.viewFilesByClassSubtitleSingle: Single session — download the generated files.
contents.viewFilesByClassEmpty: This generation has no downloadable files. It probably failed — check the full error.
contents.errorDialogHint: If the error mentions timeout or HTML from the gateway, try splitting the course into fewer classes or changing modality. If it mentions VAPID/API key, set the secrets in Supabase. For other errors, copy the text and share it with support.
contents.createAssessmentSubtitle: The content is injected as the description of the new assessment. You can then generate questions with AI from the editor.
contents.scopeFullHint: Every class is included as context. The AI receives the intro + all classes to generate the questions.
contents.scopeClassHint: Only the material of the selected class.
contents.noClassesDetected: No classes detected with `_CLASE_N` suffix. The assessment will be created using all available material as context.
contents.classesSelectedSummary: {{count}} of {{total}} classes selected. Only that material will be injected as context.
contents.classesRequired: Select at least one class, or use "All".
contents.assessmentCreatedToast: Assessment created — opening editor so you can generate questions with AI
contents.generateSessionsSubtitle: Generate {{count}} attendance session(s) and assign each class of the content. Students will see them on the course board.
contents.generateSessionsStartHint: First date to search valid days from. Sessions are scheduled on the days of the week you select.
contents.generateSessionsDaysHint: Select the days the class meets (e.g. Mon and Wed). The system walks from the start date looking for those days until N sessions are filled.
contents.generateSessionsPreviewEmpty: Pick a start date and at least one day of the week to see the computed dates.
contents.generateSessionsConflictTitle: This course already has {{count}} session(s) scheduled. What to do?
contents.generateSessionsReuseHint: No new sessions are created. Takes the first {{count}} (by date) and just assigns the content. Existing dates are kept.
contents.generateSessionsCreateHint: Inserts new sessions with the computed dates — they coexist with the existing ones.
contents.generateSessionsErrDays: Pick at least one day of the week.
contents.generateSessionsErrCount: Could not compute enough dates with the selected days.
contents.materializeSubtitle: Batch-create assessments per cut: 1 workshop + 1 exam per cut, 1 project in the last cut. Only types whose cut bucket is > 0 are proposed. Class ranges come from the already-scheduled sessions.
contents.materializeNoCutsBody: The wizard uses the cuts (with their weight and workshop/exam/project buckets) to decide what to create and how to split classes. Configure them in the course editor and come back.
contents.materializeNoSessionsBody: Without scheduled sessions for this content we can't derive the class range that falls in each cut. Schedule the sessions (auto-assigns every Class_N) and come back.
contents.materializeNoProposals: No cut has buckets > 0 for workshop/exam/project. Check the cut weights in the course editor.
contents.materializeNoClassesInRange: This cut has no classes in its date range — the proposal is disabled. Adjust the cut dates or schedule more sessions.
contents.assignDialogSubtitle: Map each content class to an attendance session. Students will see the material on the course board, on the session date.
contents.assignNoSessions: The course has no sessions scheduled yet. Create sessions from Attendance to assign content.
contents.derivedSessionsHint: Attendance sessions that have this content assigned (scheduled via "Schedule course sessions" or "Assign to sessions").
contents.derivedExamsHint: Exams created with this content as context (via "Create assessment").
contents.derivedWorkshopsHint: Workshops created with this content as context (via "Create assessment").
contents.derivedProjectsHint: Projects created with this content as context (via "Create assessment").
contents.previewNoBody: This file has no extracted body to render inline. Download it to view.
contents.regenerateDialogFullSubtitle: Tweak the topic or instructions before re-launching generation. The current content will be replaced.
contents.regenerateDialogClassSubtitle: Edit topic or instructions to refine ONLY this class. The intro and other classes stay untouched.
contents.regenerateQueuedClass: Class {{class}} regeneration queued. It will appear in "AI Queue → Generations".
contents.regenerateQueuedFull: Regeneration queued. It will appear in "AI Queue → Generations" until processed.
contents.regenerateSessionInvalid: Invalid session. Please reload the page.
contents.classNoSessionHint: This class is not assigned to a course session yet. Use "Assign to sessions" or "Generate sessions" from the content menu.
contents.editExternalSubtitle: Fix the metadata of an uploaded material (without touching the files). Useful when you misclassified it — for example, you uploaded it as individual material but it's actually a full course.
contents.classTopicHint: Specific topic for this class (not the full course). The course keeps its general topic; only this class is regenerated with the new focus.
contents.classInstructionsHint: Additional notes for this class only. If left empty, the course's general instructions are used.
contents.uploadExternalSubtitle: Upload files created outside ExamLab (PDF, slides, docs) and fill in the same pedagogical metadata the AI would use. No generation is invoked — the material is just registered.
contents.uploadInstructionsHint: Free notes about this material. Saved with the content; useful if you later decide to regenerate with AI or materialize assessments.

certSettings.resolutionHelp: How each field is resolved when issuing:
certSettings.resolutionHelpChain: course override (teacher) → this panel (institution) → default text
certSettings.resolutionHelpHint: . Anything left empty here falls back to the default text. Whatever the teacher overrides for their course wins.
certSettings.title: Certificates for your institution
certSettings.titleHelpHint: These values are what the institution uses by default when issuing a certificate. Each teacher can adjust them for their own course from the course detail. What the student downloads is an immutable snapshot at the moment of issuance.

moduleVisibility.scopeGlobalBody: What you save here is the default that ALL institutions receive. Each Admin can override it for their institution from their own panel.
moduleVisibility.scopeTenantBody: What you save here applies ONLY to this institution and overlays on top of the platform global configuration. Anything you leave untouched is inherited from the global default.

help.newPasswordLeaveEmpty: Leave empty to keep the current password.
help.studentIdentityOfficialData: Institutional data that appears on official records and certificates. All fields are optional but recommended.
help.moduleVisibilityHelp: Enable or disable modules per role (3 switches on the right side of each row). The vertical order defines how they appear in the sidebar — drag a row or use the ▲▼ arrows. Order changes are saved when you press "Save order". Switches save instantly.
help.overrideCodesPurpose: If the global mode is queue but a teacher needs an AI grade right away (e.g. course closing), generate a code here and share it with them through an external channel (email/Slack). Once the teacher activates it in the app, their session stays in sync mode for window minutes.
help.windowDurationHelp: Duration of the sync window after activating the code. Falls back to async when it expires.
help.maxActivationsHelp: How many teachers (or times) can activate this same code before it is depleted. Typical: 1 = single use.
help.maxMessagesHelp: How many sync AI grades a SINGLE activation can consume before falling back to async. Protects the Gemini quota. Empty = no cap.
help.courseWeightBudgetValidation: Assign each course to a cut and configure the workshop weight. The available budget is validated independently per cut.
help.cutAssignmentGradeCalculationWorkshop: Optional. Assign the workshop to a cut so its weight counts toward the final course grade.
help.introVideosSequentialUnlockWorkshop: Ordered list of videos the student must watch before submitting the workshop. Order matters: the next video unlocks when the student finishes the previous one. If the list is empty, no video is required. YouTube/Vimeo are played via iframe (no seek control). To prevent fast-forwarding, upload an MP4 directly to the video library.
help.contentDisplayNameHint: Unique name to identify this content on the dashboard and in selectors. For example: "Week 5 — Control structures" or "Cohort 2026-I · Algorithms". It is different from the topic (what is requested from the AI).
help.contentReleaseAfterSessionHint: When enabled, the student will only see the content once the date of its assigned session arrives. Useful to avoid spoilers for workshops, exercises, or exams.
help.displayNameHint: Name you will see for this material in your list. Unique per teacher.
help.coursesTargetHint: The material will appear on the dashboard of each selected course. You can link it to more courses later by editing the content.
help.pollMulticourseHint: A single poll can apply to multiple courses. Students enrolled in ANY of the selected courses can vote. Quotas and auto-close add up enrolled students across all courses. Useful when you teach the same material in several courses and want to consolidate results.
help.pollSessionAssociationHint: If you choose a session, the poll will appear highlighted in the attendance screen of that session (teacher) and as a pending action in the student's card. Only sessions of the first selected course are listed.
help.pollCloseDatetimeHint: Date and time when the poll stops accepting responses. After that moment students can no longer vote and, depending on the results configuration, may only then see them. If you leave it empty, the poll stays open until you close it manually from the list.
help.pollAllowChangeResponseHint: When enabled, the student can change their vote while the poll remains open. If disabled, once they vote it is locked until close. Useful when the decision is final (e.g. confirming attendance).
help.pollAutoCloseAllRespondedHint: When enabled, the poll closes by itself once every enrolled student (from the linked courses) has answered at least once. Useful to avoid leaving it "open forever" after the last student picks. If any student never responds, auto-close does not trigger and you have to close it manually.
help.courseCutsDefinition: Defines how many grading cuts this course has. 0 = no cuts (the per-type weights are edited in this same dialog). If you set 1+, the per-type weights (exams / workshops / projects / attendance) are configured inside each cut below.
help.maxExamAttemptsHelp: Maximum number of times a student can take an exam in this course (useful for quizzes). When the limit is exceeded, the last attempt is recorded and the exam is marked as suspended. Each exam in the course inherits this default and can be adjusted individually from the exam editor.
help.certMessagePlaceholdersHint: You can use placeholders: {"{{student}}"}, {"{{course}}"}, {"{{grade}}"}, {"{{period}}"}, {"{{teacher}}"}, {"{{date}}"}. If empty, the default text is used.
help.providerComparison:
OpenAI: connects directly with your platform.openai.com account. Access to gpt-4o / gpt-4.1 / etc. models. Billed to your OpenAI account.

Google Gemini (direct): your own project on Google AI Studio / Vertex. Billed to your GCP account. More control over quotas and data residency.
help.startTimeTimezoneHint: Local time (Bogotá). Used when syncing with your external calendar.
help.cutSelectionHelp: The session contributes to the attendance grade of the cut you choose. If you leave it as "No cut", it stays visible but does not count toward the grade. You can reassign it later from the "Cut" column of the table.
help.recordingOptionsHelp: You can paste the recording link (Meet, Teams, Zoom, Loom…), pick a video from the platform library, or both. If you uploaded the recording to the library, the student will see it embedded in the session; if it is an external link it will be shown as a "View recording" button.
help.checkinDurationHelp: How long the window stays open before closing automatically. Default 10 min.
help.checkinRotationHelp: How often the 6-digit code rotates. Shorter = more secure, more friction if the network is slow. Default 60s.
help.rubricHelpCriteria: Criteria the AI will use to grade. Does not apply to closed/multi question types.
help.whiteboardCourseSharingHelp: By linking the whiteboard to a course, students of the course will be able to view it in read-only mode when you enable "Share with students" from the whiteboard. Without a course, the whiteboard is private (only you can see it).
help.whiteboardSessionBindingHelp: If the course has scheduled sessions, you can pin the whiteboard to a specific one so it shows up in the context of that class. You can have several whiteboards per session.
help.projectDescriptionContext: Important: this description is the global context of the project. The AI uses it when grading each question so the grades make sense as a whole and not as isolated questions. Define the purpose, scope and constraints of the project in 3-6 sentences; the deliverables go in each question, not here.
help.introVideosHelpProject: Ordered list of videos the student must watch before submitting code. Order matters: the next video unlocks when the student finishes the previous one. If the list is empty, no video is required. Only applies if the project has a "code (ZIP)" question. YouTube/Vimeo are played via iframe (no seek control). To prevent fast-forwarding, upload an MP4 directly to the video library.
help.linkedCoursesHelp: The project is published identically in every selected course — students enrolled in any of them will see it. The cut and the AI language come from the course tied to the cut you choose below.
help.cutWeightPerCourseProject: Assign each linked course to a cut and configure the weight (% of the final grade). The available budget is validated independently per cut.
help.examStatusHelp: Draft keeps it hidden from students — useful for preparing the exam without it showing up in their lists. Published shows it within the date window. Closed manually blocks new attempts.
help.examRetryModeHelp: Only applies if more than one attempt is allowed. Defines how the final grade is calculated when there are several attempts.
help.examMaxWarningsHelp: How many times the student can switch tabs, copy/paste, leave fullscreen, etc. before flagging the submission as suspicious.
help.examCutWeightPerCourseHelp: Assign each course to a cut and configure the exam weight. The available budget is validated independently per cut.
help.examCutWeightHelp: Optional. Assign the exam to a cut so its weight counts toward the final course grade.
help.sessionCountHint: Number of sessions to schedule. In "with content" mode this number is derived automatically from the content's classes.
help.sharedWhiteboardHelp: If you enable this, enrolled students can OPEN and EDIT this whiteboard from their attendance view. Changes sync live across all participants (with a small delay). When you disable it, you become the sole editor again.
help.defaultCodeProviderHelp: Defines the default engine for "code" questions. The student can choose an alternate compiler per question from the exam (in case of a transient default failure). Changes here apply immediately to subsequent executions.
help.javaGuiProviderHelp: These questions ask the student to build a UI with Swing/AWT/JavaFX. Since no console provider can render a UI, there is a separate selector. See docs/JAVA-GUI-OPTIONS.md.
help.pythonGuiProviderHelp: These questions ask the student to build a UI with tkinter. The runner executes the code server-side (AWS Lambda + Xvfb) and returns a PNG screenshot. There is no client-side equivalent: there is no Pyodide+tkinter in WebAssembly.
help.courseDefaultsHint: These values are applied when creating a course. The teacher/admin can override them per course.
help.examDefaultsHint: Values prefilled when creating an exam. Each teacher can override per exam.
help.workshopMaxAttempts: How many times a student can submit a workshop. Global default; the teacher can override it per individual workshop.
help.projectMaxAttempts: How many times a student can submit a project. Global default; the teacher can override it per project.
help.requireExamFullscreen: When enabled (recommended), exams require fullscreen and exiting it counts as a strike. Disable only for debugging/support — without fullscreen, the student may have external tools in view.
help.emailAlertThreshold: If emails sent in the last 24h exceed the threshold, all admins receive a notification. Useful to detect notification loops or unexpected activity spikes.
help.projectCodeTypeHint: In projects, Code means the student selects several source code files directly (uncompressed). The AI receives all minified files in a single prompt and grades them with the rubric and the points of this question. Diagrams and documents go in separate questions.
help.projectJavaFrameworkHint:
Swing/AWT: JDK built-in framework, supported by CheerpJ (browser) and AWS Lambda.
JavaFX: requires OpenJFX 21 (Lambda runner). Does NOT work with CheerpJ. The student's class must extends Application; the server-side wrapper calls Application.launch().
help.projectLanguageExtensionHint: The language sets which extensions the student's file selector will accept. Java → .java, Python → .py, etc. Any file with another extension is blocked before upload.
help.projectZipSingleModeHint: Sets how the student submits this code question. ON: they upload a single .zip; the server decompresses it and the AI receives every file intact (no minification, no per-file truncation). OFF: they upload multiple loose files for the language, which are minified before the AI. Both modes are supported — pick the one that fits the deliverable.
help.workshopJavaFrameworkHelp:
Swing/AWT: JDK built-in framework, supported by CheerpJ (browser) and AWS Lambda. Historical default.
JavaFX: requires OpenJFX 21 (installed in the Lambda runner). Does NOT work with CheerpJ. The student's class must extends Application; the server-side wrapper calls Application.launch() automatically.
help.codigoZipLanguageWhitelist: Whitelist of accepted extensions — Java only .java, Python only .py. Anything else (PDFs, README, configs) is rejected before upload.
help.codigoZipSingleModeToggle:
OFF (default): the student uploads N individual files in the language. They are filtered by extension and minified before AI.
ON: the student uploads ONE .zip with the whole project. The backend decompresses and the AI receives raw files without minification or truncation — ideal for small submissions where you want the AI to "see" all the code.
help.examStatusDescriptionDetail: Draft keeps it hidden from students — useful for preparing the exam without it showing up in their lists. Published shows it during the date window. Closed manually blocks it before the window ends.
help.retryModeExplanation: Only applies if more than one attempt is allowed. Defines how the final exam grade is calculated when the student takes several attempts.
help.multiChoiceExplanation: Mark all correct options. The student receives proportional points based on how many correct ones they mark (no penalty for incorrect ones).
help.minSelectionsExplanation: If set, the student MUST mark at least this many options for it to count as answered.
help.maxSelectionsExplanation: If set, the student CANNOT mark more than this many options (score = 0 if exceeded).
help.shareWithCourseHint: If you choose a course and enable "Share", enrolled students will be able to see this whiteboard in read-only mode from their courses section. Without a course, the whiteboard is private — only you can see it.
help.pollAllowChangeResponseShort: When enabled, the student can change their answer while the question stays open. Useful for the classic show-of-hands in class.
help.pollAutoCloseAllRespondedShort: When enabled, the question closes by itself once every enrolled student in the course has voted. For live sessions you usually want this disabled: the teacher typically closes it before stragglers respond.
help.gradeInputScale: The grade is entered on the item's scale (0 up to the max score of the exam/workshop/project). Decimals with comma (e.g. 4.5).
help.gradeInputScaleDeep: The grade is entered on the item's scale (0 up to the max score of the exam/workshop/project). Decimals with comma (e.g. 4.5).
help.consolidatedByCutsExplanation: Course → Σ(Cuts × weight) → Σ(Workshops, Exams, Projects, Attendance × weight). This view is read-only — weights are edited in Courses → Cuts.
help.editRoleOnly: Only edit the role/criteria of the model. The dynamic data (rubric, student answer, language, max score) are injected automatically when grading runs — no placeholders required.
help.actaImmutableRegistry: Immutable record of the course closing. Captures the cohort of enrolled students at the moment the record is generated. To print the PDF, use the "Print record" option from the actions menu.
help.programOverviewHelp: KPIs aggregated by program/level — active subjects, total courses (instances), unique students and teachers. Filter by period to see only what is active in that cycle.
help.reportScopeHelp: "Per student" = one report per student. "Per course" = a consolidated one iterating over {{#each estudiantes}}.
help.edgeFunctionSecretsDescription: Environment variables visible to Supabase edge functions. Edit API keys here (Gemini, OpenAI, OnlineCompiler, AWS Runner, etc.) without going to the Supabase dashboard or redeploying.
help.backupSnapshotsExplanation: Logical snapshots of the tables to a ZIP in Storage. The cron `db-backup-weekly` runs on Sundays at 03:05 UTC and creates one with all tables; the "Create backup" button lets you generate a manual one choosing which tables to include. There is NO restore from the UI for safety reasons — download the ZIP and apply it via SQL if needed.
help.privateUrlWarning: This URL is PRIVATE — anyone who has it can see your events. If you shared it by mistake press "Regenerate URL".
help.retentionHelp: Defines how many days to keep records of each severity level. 0 = no limit (default). Purging is triggered by a monthly cron configured in Supabase.

Each period averages workshops, exams, projects and attendance using the weights set by the teacher. Components without data do not penalize: their weights are distributed among the others.

aiQueue.gateDescNoQueue: The global AI mode is set to queue (batch). This action CANNOT be queued (no worker for it). To proceed, activate an immediate AI code or cancel.
aiQueue.gateHintActivate: Activate immediate AI: enter the administrator code to open a short synchronous window.
aiQueue.gateHintQueue: Continue in queue: the job is queued and processed when the worker runs. No immediate quota cost.

E.g. dates Jun 10 and Jun 11, window 9:00–10:00, every 15 min → 8 slots (4 per date). When you generate, those dates become slots and are removed from the list so duplicates aren't generated. hc_routesIndex.metaDescription: Multi-institution, AI and proctoring to manage courses, exams, workshops, projects, attendance and certificates. For Administrators, Teachers and Students.
hc_routesIndex.ogDescription: Courses, AI-powered exams, group workshops, projects with defense, QR attendance and certificates — all in a single platform.
hc_routesIndex.heroSubtitle: Courses, AI-powered exams, group workshops, projects with defense, QR attendance and certificates — for Administrators, Teachers and Students in a single platform.
hc_routesIndex.featureRolesDesc: Admin, Teacher, Student and SuperAdmin with granular permissions enforced at the database level (RLS). Each institution with its own brand, users and isolated data.
hc_routesIndex.featureAiDesc: Generate questions and rubrics with Gemini. Grade open-ended answers, source code and complete projects with feedback explained to the student.
hc_routesIndex.featureProctoringDesc: Full screen, copy blocking, monitored tab switching and detection of AI-generated answers. Similarity analysis between submissions.
hc_routesIndex.featureCompilerDesc: Monaco editor with Java, Python, JavaScript and C execution. Java GUI with screenshot via AWS Lambda. Per-question override for resilience.
hc_routesIndex.featureAttendanceDesc: Weights per cut, attendance integrated into the final grade calculation and self-service check-in with a rotating TOTP code that the teacher projects.
hc_routesIndex.featureOfflineDesc: Installable as an app. Answers saved locally in IndexedDB and automatic synchronization when the connection is restored. Native push notifications.
